title: Multi‑brand Gift Cards

narrative: The user wants a comprehensive overview of French multi‑brand (multi‑sign) gift card solutions, such as Illinocado, UpCadhoc, CADO, and the Galeries Lafayette e‑gift card. Documents describing these multi‑brand voucher programs, their partner networks, usage maps, and user management features are considered relevant. Pages that only discuss single‑brand gift cards (e.g., Decathlon, Old Campeur) or unrelated promotional content are not relevant.

title: Traditional Lamb Tagine
description: What is a classic Moroccan lamb tagine recipe that uses traditional spices and vegetables?
narrative: The user wants a genuine, traditional lamb tagine recipe that includes typical Moroccan ingredients such as lamb, olive oil, saffron, coriander, mint, potatoes, tomatoes, olives, and preserved (candied) lemon, along with step‑by‑step cooking instructions (sautéing, layering, baking/slow‑cooking). Documents that provide such a recipe and mention these core ingredients and methods are relevant. Sources that describe variations with other vegetables or fruits (e.g., butternut squash, prunes, dates, apricots) or that lack the traditional ingredient list are not relevant.

narrative: The user is looking for detailed guidance on early or "new" potatoes, covering proper storage conditions, the delicate handling required for the thin‑skinned tubers, recipes for cooking them (e.g., sautéed, pan‑fried, rissolée, or oven‑roasted shot potatoes), and safety information about eating sprouted potatoes (toxicity of solanine, removal of sprouts/green parts, and risk assessment). title: Back-to-Work Bonus
description: What are the current eligibility criteria and application steps for a French RSA/SSA beneficiary to receive the back‑to‑work bonus after resuming employment?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive information on the French "prime de retour à l'emploi" (back‑to‑work bonus). Relevant documents should explain who can claim the bonus (e.g., RSA, SSA, IPA recipients), the minimum duration and amount of work required (e.g., at least four consecutive months or 78 hours per month, CDI/CDD length), required supporting documents (employment contract, pay slips), timing of the request (within a few months of resumption), the fixed amount (€1,000) and frequency limits (once every 18 months). The user is also interested in whether the bonus can be claimed after only one month of work, how departmental variations such as APRE (personalised back‑to‑work assistance) affect eligibility, and whether the scheme is still in force.
